The Momentum Balance in HYCOM  
 
By Rainer Bleck, 27 February 2000 
 
Updated for HYCOM 1.0/2.0 by George Halliwell, 27 August 2001 
 
 
General Remarks 
 
Except for the vertical diffusion term, the momentum equation in HYCOM is the same as in 
MICOM with a few exceptions. The momentum balance has, of course, been modified to handle 
horizontally varying density in all model layers. Surface momentum flux accelerates only the 
fluid in layer 1 except when the Kraus-Turner mixed layer model Is used, when the momentum 
change is distributed over the full mixed layer depth that was calculated during the previous time 
step. 
 
The most significant modification of the momentum equation in hybrid vertical coordinates 
concerns the horizontal pressure gradient force (PGF), which is discussed in detail below. 
 
The Horizontal Pressure Gradient Force in Generalized Coordinates

catalog_entry.summary: This paper discusses modifications to the momentum equation in HYCOM, focusing on the horizontal pressure gradient force in generalized coordinates and its implications for vortex spinup and spindown.
